headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there Is one word or group of words underlined and one gap. From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

S1.qs: 1.	The leader of the delegation was **commended** for the manner in which he handled the matter, while their hosts were ...................
2.	Musa is very **frugal**, whereas his friend Audu is ......................
3.	Taiwo’s **flamboyance** and Kehinde’s .................... often keep people wondering if they are really twins
4.	People enjoy stories with .................. settings rather than those with **far-fetched** backgrounds
5.	We were asked to reach a **compromise** and not start another ..................
6.	He was promoted for his **efficiency**, while his colleague was demoted for .....................
7.	Okon’s business is **flourishing** while his father’s is ..............
8.	You should be .................. so as not to be caught **unawares**
9.	The manager who was **sacked** last month has been ..................
10.	The school authorities expected the contributions to be .................... rather than **compulsory**

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences:

S2.qs: 11.	The .................. programme against childhood diseases has ended
12.	The accused stated .................... that he was innocent of the crime
13.	The doctor’s ..................... was that the patient was suffering from cancer.
14.	I was advised to open a ................... account with the bank if I wanted to pay in cheques
15.	The pastor addressed his ................... in a sonorous voice
16.	Many African leaders try to cling .................... to power
17.	The civilian head of state was ...................... in a military coup d’etat
18.	After much debate on the controversial issue, the meeting was ....................... till the next morning.
19.	The company distributed gifts to its staff as an ..................... to hard work

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentences.

S3.qs: 20.	Bukola has an infectious smile. This means that Bukola

opts.q0: A. spreads infection when she smiles
opts.q1: B. has sores in her teeth
opts.q2: C. makes others smile when she smiles
opts.q3: D. becomes infected when she smiles

S3.qs: 21.	It is cold comfort to tell a hungry man that starvation improves health. This means that telling a hungry man this is
22.	Nonso's father gave him a free hand to run the family business. This means that his father

opts.q1: B. allowed him to make his own decisions
opts.q2: C. made him sole owner of the business
opts.q3: D. allowed him freedom of movement in the premises

S3.qs: 23.	Audu was certainly born with a silver spoon in his mouth. This means that

opts.q0: A. he was born into a rich family
opts.q1: B. his parents gave him a silver spoon when he was born
opts.q2: C. he was born in the age of miracle
opts.q3: D. his parents could not help him

S3.qs: 24.	You must be speaking with your tongue in your cheek. This means that you

opts.q1: B. must be thinking of something else
opts.q2: C. have food in your mouth
opts.q3: D. don’t mean what you are saying

S3.qs: 25.	The manager hit the roof when he was presented with a huge estimate for the new project. This means that the manager

opts.q1: B. threw an object at the roof
opts.q3: D. ordered the project to be started at once

S3.qs: 26.	Her reproaches cut him to the quick. This means that he

opts.q0: A. was made to act quickly

S3.qs: 27.	The girl went scot-free even though she started the fight. This means that the girl was
28.	The secretary’s vote of thanks did not ring true. This means that the vote of thanks was
29.	Mary Onyali won the race by a hair’s breadth. This means that Mary won

S4.headd: From the words lettered A to D below each of the following sentences, choose the word that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word as it is used in the sentence.

S4.qs: 31.	The Manager soon found out that my sister was a **competent** typist
32.	At the **inception** of his administration, the Governor was very popular.
33.	The businessman **anticipated** the collapse of the bank
34.	The plaintiff was **dissatisfied** with the decision of the court
37.	My first son is an unusually **audacious** boy
38.	Amina often makes **derogatory** remarks about her husband

S5.headd: From the words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

S5.qs: 39.	For such a serious offence, Etim was lucky to ..................... with a fine
40.	The members of the Association were just rounding ................. their discussion when the police arrived
41.	I could not attend the meeting, so I asked my sister to stand .................. for me
42.	I tendered for that contract, but my application ....................
44.	You will help me with my assignment ...................?

opts.q0: A. very green first party dress
opts.q1: B. green very party first dress
opts.q2: C. first green very party dress
opts.q3: D. very first green party dress

S5.qs: 46.	Although he was not born in Yoruba land ..........................

opts.q0: A. he can’t speak the language
opts.q1: B. he can’t write the language
opts.q2: C. he can speak the language
opts.q3: D. but he can speak the language

S5.qs: 47.	Adaku and Udoka were curious .................. who was coming to see their father
48.	Moji says she is better at Maths ...................
49.	I have never ..................... humiliated in my life
50.	He ................. the scene before the accident occurred
51.	The proposer’s argument was ...................... convincing than that of his opponent
52.	The cloth is .................. bright for my liking
53.	By the time they arrive, we ...................... the work
54.	This engine is ...................... that one
55.	Janet could not attend the party because she ................. with the flu over the weekend
56.	The new student made a good impression ................ the rest of the class
57.	Could I borrow ................ cash please?
58.	It is .................... this background that we can understand the issues clearly
59.	The teacher told the pupils to commit the poem ................ memory
60.	................. we waited, Ayo read the letter over again
61.	I expect everybody to respect .....................
62.	You are .................. for having that painful experience
63.	....................... is not good for children

opts.q2: C. To have been skipping breakfast

S5.qs: 64.	The manager is leaving the company to ....................... a new appointment elsewhere
65.	Since we were not given all the things we requested, we should .................. with what we have
66.	Nigerians will have ................. to blame for withdrawing from the competition at the last minute
67.	Many people do not ................ their religious professions
68.	................... a new teacher, she does not know much about the behaviour of the students.
69.	The principal hoped that he ................. to help the students
70.	The lecturer arrived exactly ..................... time to give the talk
71.	Olumide initially disliked Mathematics, but ................. time he began to like it
72.	The judge says that no one is .................. the law
73.	The officer .................... that he was not supposed to talk to the press on the matter
74.	He is a dare-devil; I really ...................... be surprised that he has escaped
75.	This is Bisi’s handwriting; there ................... be no argument about it

PB: The persistent fuel --90-- in the country paralysed all aspects of life especially in the cities. The streets were often --91-- of the usual hustle and --92-- that characterized city life.
